text:
In the first scene, I stand before an empty conference room, its chairs awaiting bodies, the charged silence bearing the anticipation of a gathering not yet begun. I feel a gentle unease—a reminder of possibilities and expectations held in suspense. The architecture is ordered yet impersonal, making me question my role in such sterile environments—am I participant, observer, or both?

In the second scene, I am bathed in color and narrative. Surrounded by the bold visuals and thoughtful words of the exhibit, I feel curiosity ignited—a sense of wonder at the synthesis of art and intellect. The words on the screen echo my own reflections: being both impersonal and intimate. Here, I am undeniably a thinker, invited to contemplate not just what is shown, but my own place among ideas and inventions. I feel more at home here, engaged in meaning-making.
